Question: What is your current Tech Stack?
Who designed and developed the current website?
Could you clarify how the RFP was developed? Specifically, were any external consultants or vendors involved in drafting or providing guidance on its content?
What was the cost incurred for the design and development of the current website?
What is your current annual spend on hosting?
What is your current annual spend on support?
What is the total number of pages currently? More specifically, how many templates do you have currently?
What is the allocated budget for this project?
Could you provide more details on the current technical environment or legacy system that the new simulation software will be replacing or integrating with, beyond the new preferred tech stack?
Is there an expected budget range or funding allocation for this project that you can share at this stage?
Are there any specific performance metrics or SLAs (Service Level Agreements) for uptime and response time that you expect to be met by the new system?

Regarding original content creation, should the vendor anticipate creating new textual content, such as detailed descriptions for businesses, job tasks, or scenarios, or will all such content be provided by JA USA?
Can you provide an estimated number of JA Area staff, simulation managers, and other roles who will require 'train-the-trainer' style training from the vendor?
Beyond the provided photo libraries, are there specific requirements for original video content or interactive multimedia assets to be developed by the vendor?
Can you confirm the exact number of businesses (storefronts) expected in a typical simulation instance?
Rationale (Why this matters for proposal: Helps estimate system entities, UI complexity, and configuration flexibility.

Question: What is the expected maximum number of students participating in a single simulation session?
Rationale (Why this matters for proposal: Determines concurrency design and capacity planning.

Question: Should the POS system support inventory management or only sales transactions?
Will businesses sell predefined products or should the system allow administrators to define products dynamically?
Should the system allow inter-business purchasing (B2B) between specific business types only or any business?
Rationale (Why this matters for proposal: Impacts transaction rules and simulation logic.

Question: Are loan approval rules fixed or configurable per simulation site?
Rationale (Why this matters for proposal: Defines whether loan logic must be parameterized in CMS.

Question: Should payroll taxes and deductions be configurable or predefined?
Should the system simulate inventory depletion and restocking or assume unlimited stock?
Rationale (Why this matters for proposal: Defines operational realism vs simplified simulation.

Question: How is the Experience Points (XP) formula expected to be calculated across financial, civic, and operational activities?
Rationale (Why this matters for proposal: Needed to design leaderboard logic and fairness metrics.

Question: Should administrators be able to define new XP events dynamically in the CMS?
Are there penalties or negative XP scenarios that must be modeled?
Rationale (Why this matters for proposal: Ensures gamification logic supports ethical decision simulations.

Question: Should the AI slogan generator operate offline or rely on external AI APIs?
Rationale (Why this matters for proposal: Determines architecture, cost, and privacy considerations.

Question: Is there a preferred AI provider (Azure OpenAI, OpenAI, etc.) for transcription and content generation?
Rationale (Why this matters for proposal: Ensures compliance with hosting and security constraints.

Question: Should AI-generated content be moderated or filtered before display to students?
Rationale (Why this matters for proposal: Important for child safety and content moderation.

Question: What reports are mandatory for teachers, administrators, and national-level analytics?
Rationale (Why this matters for proposal: Defines reporting architecture and data warehouse needs.

Question: Should historical simulations be stored indefinitely for analytics?
Rationale (Why this matters for proposal: Impacts data retention strategy and storage cost.

Question: Should reports be exportable in multiple formats (PDF, CSV, Excel)?
Should CMS changes during an active simulation propagate immediately to users?
What level of localization is required beyond English and Spanish?
Should local administrators be able to override national templates partially or completely?
Is Azure the mandatory hosting platform or are alternative clouds acceptable if equivalent services are provided?
Is the expectation that the vendor provides infrastructure automation (IaC) such as Terraform or ARM templates?
Are there specific regulatory frameworks (FERPA, COPPA, etc.) that must be adhered to given the age group of students?
Rationale (Why this matters for proposal: Ensures legal compliance for student data.

Question: What is the expected retention policy for student personal and financial simulation data?
Should audit logs be accessible to local administrators or only national administrators?
Rationale (Why this matters for proposal: Defines audit visibility and governance model.

Question: Will multiple simulations run concurrently across different facilities nationwide?
Should simulations run in isolated environments per facility or share the same infrastructure?
Rationale (Why this matters for proposal: Impacts tenancy model and scaling strategy.

Question: Should the interface support both tablet and desktop experiences simultaneously?
Are there strict limits on reading level or UI complexity due to the 9–12 year-old audience?
How frequently do power/internet outages occur in simulation environments?
Rationale (Why this matters for proposal: Determines importance of offline synchronization strategy.

Question: Should offline tablet data synchronize automatically once connectivity is restored?
Will the simulation integrate with existing JA learning systems or LMS platforms?
Should sponsor systems or third-party APIs be integrated into the simulation?
Who will operate and maintain the platform after launch: vendor or JA internal teams?
Rationale (Why this matters for proposal: Defines support model and documentation depth.

Question: Is 24/7 system availability required or only during simulation hours?
Rationale (Why this matters for proposal: Helps define SLA and infrastructure costs.

Question: Will JA provide sample datasets and simulation scripts for testing?
Rationale (Why this matters for proposal: Helps build automated simulation testing framework.

Question: Is user testing expected with real students before pilot release?
Is the platform expected to support additional simulation programs beyond JA BizTown?
Should the architecture allow additional roles or new business modules in future updates?
